name: Calculate Monthly Churn Rate
description: Analyzes customer purchase history to compute monthly churn rate. Identifies customers who made purchases in one month but not the next. Essential for understanding customer retention and predicting revenue.
sql_template:
WITH monthly_customers AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month,
    customer_id
  FROM orders
  WHERE order_date >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND total > 0
  GROUP BY 1, 2
),
churn_analysis AS (
  SELECT 
    curr.month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT curr.customer_id) AS active_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT prev.customer_id) AS retained_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT curr.customer_id) - COUNT(DISTINCT prev.customer_id) AS churned_customers
  FROM monthly_customers curr
  LEFT JOIN monthly_customers prev 
    ON prev.customer_id = curr.customer_id 
    AND prev.month = DATE_TRUNC('month', curr.month - INTERVAL '1 month')
  GROUP BY curr.month
)
SELECT 
  month,
  active_customers,
  retained_customers,
  churned_customers,
  ROUND(100.0 * churned_customers / NULLIF(active_customers, 0), 2) AS churn_rate_pct
FROM churn_analysis
ORDER BY month DESC
LIMIT {{limit}}

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Number of Months
parameters.description: How many recent months to show

author: Sery Team
example_output:
Returns a table with columns:
- `month`: Month (YYYY-MM-01)
- `active_customers`: Count of customers who ordered this month
- `retained_customers`: Count of customers who also ordered last month
- `churned_customers`: Count of customers who ordered last month but not this month
- `churn_rate_pct`: Churn rate as percentage (0-100)
